visa_types.name: Study
visa_types.documents_required:
For information about the documents required, please visit the website of the Norway Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explains-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application) and go to the website of The Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/).
Please be advised that it is a requirement that all documents are submitted in original in the A4 format. Any government issued documents that you provide originals for (Nikah nama, birth certificates etc) must be accompanied by a copy. Any documents not in A4 size need to be copied into A4. The copies will have to be of very good quality with no staples, paper clips or glue as they will be scanned by the Embassy.
We stress the importance of submitting all the relevant documents. Missing documents may lead to a rejection or longer processing time. If you for any reason are not able to submit any of the documents, please write a short statement explaining this.
Applicants who are seeking to live with their spouse in Norway must also complete one of the following questionnaires and attach it with their applications:
- Questions about the relationship between spouses from UAE in family immigration cases
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi, which is responsible for the further examination.
Applying with children?
Please note that the Embassy of Norway will not be able to process the visa application if the child's photo on the passport is outdated or does not correspond with the recent photo enclosed.
visa_types.visa_fees:
When you apply for a visa or residence permit to Norway, you must pay the fee online when registering your application at the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/). The below fee information is therefore provided for information purposes only.
Please note that applicants will also be charged a service fee of EUR 20 or QAR 84 per application which must be paid in cash when you attend the visa application centre. The service fee is liable to change in line with the European Commission exchange rate.

[nor > qat > en > study]
Visa fees can only be paid with a credit card through the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).
These fees are effective from 1 January 2018.
visa_types.processing_time: For the latest information on processing times in Family Immigration applications, please go to the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/guide-to-case-processing-times-in-family-immigration-cases/).
visa_types.forms:
The application form for Norway’s visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/)
Proof of consent by one parent if you are travelling with the other
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to study in Norway, you must apply for a residence permit for studies. This is also called a study permit. You can apply for a residence permit to study at a university college or university, attend bible school or folk high school. You can also apply for a study permit if you are a skilled worker and are going to study Norwegian or take education to be recognized in Norway.
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information or visit the websites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and The Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/).
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi, which is responsible for the further examination.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to study in Norway, you must apply for a residence permit for studies. This is also called a study permit. You can apply for a residence permit to study at a university college or university, attend bible school or folk high school. You can also apply for a study permit if you are a skilled worker and are going to study Norwegian or take education to be recognized in Norway.
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information or visit the websites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and The Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/).
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi, which is responsible for the further examination.

## Visa Fees
When you apply for a visa or residence permit to Norway, you must pay the fee online when registering your application at the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/). The below fee information is therefore provided for information purposes only.
Please note that applicants will also be charged a service fee of EUR 20 or QAR 84 per application which must be paid in cash when you attend the visa application centre. The service fee is liable to change in line with the European Commission exchange rate.

[nor > qat > en > study]
Visa fees can only be paid with a credit card through the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).
These fees are effective from 1 January 2018.

## Documents Required
For information about the documents required, please visit the website of the Norway Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explains-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application) and go to the website of The Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/).
Please be advised that it is a requirement that all documents are submitted in original in the A4 format. Any government issued documents that you provide originals for (Nikah nama, birth certificates etc) must be accompanied by a copy. Any documents not in A4 size need to be copied into A4. The copies will have to be of very good quality with no staples, paper clips or glue as they will be scanned by the Embassy.
We stress the importance of submitting all the relevant documents. Missing documents may lead to a rejection or longer processing time. If you for any reason are not able to submit any of the documents, please write a short statement explaining this.
Applicants who are seeking to live with their spouse in Norway must also complete one of the following questionnaires and attach it with their applications:
- Questions about the relationship between spouses from UAE in family immigration cases
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi, which is responsible for the further examination.
Applying with children?
Please note that the Embassy of Norway will not be able to process the visa application if the child's photo on the passport is outdated or does not correspond with the recent photo enclosed.

## Photo Specifications
The photo must:
- be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- be printed on high quality photo paper with high solution
- be in colour
- be 35–40 mm in width
- be maximum 6 months old and resemble the applicant
- be taken with the applicant facing the camera directly, against a light background
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face must occupy 70–80 % of the picture.
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair must not cover the eyes.
Regarding glasses:
Glasses are allowed as long as the eyes are clearly visible.
- the glasses must not be coloured
- the rim must not cover any part of the eyes
- there must be no reflection in the glasses
Regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed, provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- shadows on the face due to the head-dress are not allowed
- Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. A photo booth for meeting these requirements is available at the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
For the latest information on processing times in Family Immigration applications, please go to the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/guide-to-case-processing-times-in-family-immigration-cases/).

## Download Forms
The application form for Norway’s visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/)
Proof of consent by one parent if you are travelling with the other

We stress the importance of submitting all the relevant documents. Missing documents may lead to a rejection or longer processing time. If you for any reason are not able to submit any of the documents, please write a short statement explaining this.
Applicants who are seeking to live with their spouse in Norway must also complete one of the following questionnaires and attach it with their applications:
- Questions about the relationship between spouses from UAE in family immigration cases
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi, which is responsible for the further examination.

## Photo Specifications

- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair must not cover the eyes.
Regarding glasses:
Glasses are allowed as long as the eyes are clearly visible.
- the glasses must not be coloured
- the rim must not cover any part of the eyes
- there must be no reflection in the glasses
Regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed, provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- shadows on the face due to the head-dress are not allowed
- Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. A photo booth for meeting these requirements is available at the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
For the latest information on processing times in Family Immigration applications, please go to the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/guide-to-case-processing-times-in-family-immigration-cases/).

visa_types.name: Short Term Visas
visa_types.overview:
Applications for short stay visas are accepted at the Visa Application Centre through prior appointments.
All applications have to be made in person by the applicant due to the biometric enrolment process.
All applicants must fully comply with relevant documentation required for respective visa categories.
You may either submit your visa application at the Embassy of Norway in UAE or at the nearest VFS Global Norway Visa Application Centre. Those applying for short-stay Schengen visas (for business, tourism, leisure) are advised to submit their application at the Norway Visa Application Centre.
The convention implementing the Schengen Agreement came into force on 26 March 1995. The Schengen countries require visitors to obtain a common visa, which permits entry into the Area. The Schengen countries have reached agreements with each other on entry requirements and a common Visa Application Form
visa_types.full_text:
Applications for short stay visas are accepted at the Visa Application Centre through prior appointments.
All applications have to be made in person by the applicant due to the biometric enrolment process.
All applicants must fully comply with relevant documentation required for respective visa categories.
You may either submit your visa application at the Embassy of Norway in UAE or at the nearest VFS Global Norway Visa Application Centre. Those applying for short-stay Schengen visas (for business, tourism, leisure) are advised to submit their application at the Norway Visa Application Centre.
The convention implementing the Schengen Agreement came into force on 26 March 1995. The Schengen countries require visitors to obtain a common visa, which permits entry into the Area. The Schengen countries have reached agreements with each other on entry requirements and a common Visa Application Form

visa_types.processing_time:
Normal case processing times
Applications for au pair permits will be processed within eight weeks of the application being submitted to the police or a foreign service mission.
If it will take longer, we will notify you by email or SMS.
Which cases take longer to process?
In some cases, we need more information or documentation, or there may be things that we want to look into more closely. We would not do so if we did not believe that it is absolutely necessary in order to make a correct decision in the case.
What can you do to help your case being processed as quickly as possible?
Make sure that your application is completed correctly and in full.
Make sure that you enclose all the necessary documents, see our checklists.
Make sure that your contact information, as well as your host family’s, is up-to-date.
It is also a good idea to make sure that your host family in Norway has power of attorney from you, so that we can give important information to the

Overview

If you wish to come to Norway to work, you need a residence permit. You must normally have found a job first. What residence permit you should apply for depends on your competence and the type of work you will be doing in Norway.
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information or visit the websites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and The Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/).
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi, which is responsible for the further examination.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

If you wish to come to Norway to work, you need a residence permit. You must normally have found a job first. What residence permit you should apply for depends on your competence and the type of work you will be doing in Norway.
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information or visit the websites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and The Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/).
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi, which is responsible for the further examination.

## Visa Fees
When you apply for a visa or residence permit to Norway, you must pay the fee online when registering your application at the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/). The below fee information is therefore provided for information purposes only.
Please note that applicants will also be charged a service fee of EUR 20 or QAR 84 per application which must be paid in cash when you attend the visa application centre. The service fee is liable to change in line with the European Commission exchange rate.

[nor > qat > en > work Immigration]
Visa fees can only be paid with a credit card through the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/).
These fees are effective from 1 January 2018.

## Documents Required

For information about the documents required, please visit the website of the Norway Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explains-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application) and go to the website of The Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/).
Please be advised that it is a requirement that all documents are submitted in original in the A4 format. Any government issued documents that you provide originals for (Nikah nama, birth certificates etc) must be accompanied by a copy. Any documents not in A4 size need to be copied into A4. The copies will have to be of very good quality with no staples, paper clips or glue as they will be scanned by the Embassy.
We stress the importance of submitting all the relevant documents. Missing documents may lead to a rejection or longer processing time. If you for any reason are not able to submit any of the documents, please write a short statement explaining this.
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi, which is responsible for the further examination.
Applying with children?
Please note that the Embassy of Norway will not be able to process the visa application if the child's photo on the passport is outdated or does not correspond with the recent photo enclosed.

## Photo Specifications
The photo must:
- be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- be printed on high quality photo paper with high solution
- be in colour
- be 35–40 mm in width
- be maximum 6 months old and resemble the applicant
- be taken with the applicant facing the camera directly, against a light background
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face must occupy 70–80 % of the picture.
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair must not cover the eyes.
Regarding glasses:
Glasses are allowed as long as the eyes are clearly visible.
- the glasses must not be coloured
- the rim must not cover any part of the eyes
- there must be no reflection in the glasses
Regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed, provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- shadows on the face due to the head-dress are not allowed
- Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. A photo booth for meeting these requirements is available at the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
Normal case processing times
Applications for au pair permits will be processed within eight weeks of the application being submitted to the police or a foreign service mission.
If it will take longer, we will notify you by email or SMS.
Which cases take longer to process?
In some cases, we need more information or documentation, or there may be things that we want to look into more closely. We would not do so if we did not believe that it is absolutely necessary in order to make a correct decision in the case.
What can you do to help your case being processed as quickly as possible?
Make sure that your application is completed correctly and in full.
Make sure that you enclose all the necessary documents, see our checklists.
Make sure that your contact information, as well as your host family’s, is up-to-date.

visa_types.name: Visit Family or Friends
visa_types.documents_required:
Please click here for the checklist for the required documents. For more information see link (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/visitors-visa/).
Applying with children?
Please note that the Embassy of Norway will not be able to process the visa application if the child's photo on the passport is outdated or does not correspond with the recent photo enclosed.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[nor > qat > en > Tourism > Visa Fees]
Please Note:
- Please refer to the UDI website for visa fee.
- Applicant shall be charged a service charge of EUR 20 per application
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay (Euro 18 or QAR 76) to cover the Resubmission Courier
- Service charge will be paid online.
- Any additional services will be paid at the Visa Application Centre by Cash (QAR) or Card only.
visa_types.processing_time:
You can now apply for a Schengen visa to Norway six months ahead of date of travel, but it is required to apply at least 15 days before your planned travel date. In this regard, we stress that you should not buy your tickets and pay hotel bookings before you know that a visa has been granted. For more information click here
The processing time for applications is normally up to 15 calendar days after the embassy or consulate has received the application. The decision period may be extended to 45 days when further consideration is needed.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
visa_types.forms:
The application form for Norway’s visa needs to be filled online.
Note: While filling the application form online the Country you applying from must be selected as United Arab Emirates and the visa fees must be paid online.
To launch an online application form, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/)
Proof of consent by one parent if you are travelling with the other
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Business Visa is granted to those visiting the Schengen Area for business purposes.
This visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only nationals who are legal residents in the Qatar can apply for a visa at the Application Centre.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid online at the time of completing the online application.
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Business Visa is granted to those visiting the Schengen Area for business purposes.
This visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only nationals who are legal residents in the Qatar can apply for a visa at the Application Centre.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid online at the time of completing the online application.
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.

## Visa Fees

[nor > qat > en > Tourism > Visa Fees]
Please Note:
- Please refer to the UDI website for visa fee.
- Applicant shall be charged a service charge of EUR 20 per application
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay (Euro 18 or QAR 76) to cover the Resubmission Courier
- Service charge will be paid online.
- Any additional services will be paid at the Visa Application Centre by Cash (QAR) or Card only.

## Documents Required
Please click here for the checklist for the required documents. For more information see link (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/visitors-visa/).
Applying with children?
Please note that the Embassy of Norway will not be able to process the visa application if the child's photo on the passport is outdated or does not correspond with the recent photo enclosed.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself. The photograph should be in colour and:
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling (without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).
regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed, provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- shadows on the face due to the head-dress are not allowed
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph provided does not meet these requirements, your application will be considered incomplete. A photo booth is available at the Application Centre to help you click photographs meeting these requirements.

## Processing Time
You can now apply for a Schengen visa to Norway six months ahead of date of travel, but it is required to apply at least 15 days before your planned travel date. In this regard, we stress that you should not buy your tickets and pay hotel bookings before you know that a visa has been granted. For more information click here
The processing time for applications is normally up to 15 calendar days after the embassy or consulate has received the application. The decision period may be extended to 45 days when further consideration is needed.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
The application form for Norway’s visa needs to be filled online.
Note: While filling the application form online the Country you applying from must be selected as United Arab Emirates and the visa fees must be paid online.
To launch an online application form, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/)
Proof of consent by one parent if you are travelling with the other

visa_types.name: Residence Permits
visa_types.overview:
Residence Permits
For Norway, all permits that require a stay for longer than 90 days are called residence permits. The grounds for applying a residence permit can be work, family reunion, studies etc.
Please note that if you plan to work in Norway you will, as a main rule, need a residence permit even if the stay is planned to be shorter than 90 days.
To read about the different residence permits and requirements please see the webpages of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/). By entering general information about your nationality and the reason for applying you will get specific information on what documents to present, case processing times etc.
visa_types.full_text:
## Residence Permits
For Norway, all permits that require a stay for longer than 90 days are called residence permits. The grounds for applying a residence permit can be work, family reunion, studies etc.
Please note that if you plan to work in Norway you will, as a main rule, need a residence permit even if the stay is planned to be shorter than 90 days.
To read about the different residence permits and requirements please see the webpages of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/). By entering general information about your nationality and the reason for applying you will get specific information on what documents to present, case processing times etc.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Business Visa is granted to those visiting the Schengen Area for business purposes.
This visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only nationals who are legal residents in the Qatar can apply for a visa at the Application Centre.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid online at the time of completing the online application.
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Business Visa is granted to those visiting the Schengen Area for business purposes.
This visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States
Only nationals who are legal residents in the Qatar can apply for a visa at the Application Centre.
Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid online at the time of completing the online application.
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be:
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.

## Visa Fees

[nor > qat > en > Tourism > Visa Fees]
Please Note:
- Please refer to the UDI website for visa fee.
- Applicant shall be charged a service charge of EUR 20 per application
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay (Euro 18 or QAR 76) to cover the Resubmission Courier
- Service charge will be paid online.
- Any additional services will be paid at the Visa Application Centre by Cash (QAR) or Card only.

## Documents Required
Please click here for the checklist for the required documents. For more information see link (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/visitors-visa/).
Applying with children?
Please note that the Embassy of Norway will not be able to process the visa application if the child's photo on the passport is outdated or does not correspond with the recent photo enclosed.

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself. The photograph should be in colour and:
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling (without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background).
regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed, provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- shadows on the face due to the head-dress are not allowed
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph provided does not meet these requirements, your application will be considered incomplete. A photo booth is available at the Application Centre to help you click photographs meeting these requirements.

## Processing Time
You can now apply for a Schengen visa to Norway six months ahead of date of travel, but it is required to apply at least 15 days before your planned travel date. In this regard, we stress that you should not buy your tickets and pay hotel bookings before you know that a visa has been granted. For more information click here
The processing time for applications is normally up to 15 calendar days after the embassy or consulate has received the application. The decision period may be extended to 45 days when further consideration is needed.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
The application form for Norway’s visa needs to be filled online.
Note: While filling the application form online the Country you applying from must be selected as United Arab Emirates and the visa fees must be paid online.
To launch an online application form, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/)
Proof of consent by one parent if you are travelling with the other

visa_types.name: AU Pair
visa_types.documents_required:
For information about the documents required, please visit the website of the Norway Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explains-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application) and go to the website of The Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/).
Please be advised that it is a requirement that all documents are submitted in original in the A4 format. Any government issued documents that you provide originals for (Nikah nama, birth certificates etc) must be accompanied by a copy. Any documents not in A4 size need to be copied into A4. The copies will have to be of very good quality with no staples, paper clips or glue as they will be scanned by the Embassy.
We stress the importance of submitting all the relevant documents. Missing documents may lead to a rejection or longer processing time. If you for any reason are not able to submit any of the documents, please write a short statement explaining this.
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi, which is responsible for the further examination.
Applying with children?
Please note that the Embassy of Norway will not be able to process the visa application if the child's photo on the passport is outdated or does not correspond with the recent photo enclosed.
visa_types.visa_fees:
When you apply for a visa or residence permit to Norway, you must pay the fee online when registering your application at the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/). The below fee information is therefore provided for information purposes only.
Please note that applicants will also be charged a service fee of EUR 20 or QAR 84 per application which must be paid in cash when you attend the visa application centre. The service fee is liable to change in line with the European Commission exchange rate.

[nor > qat > en > study]
Visa fees can only be paid with a credit card through the Application Portal Norway.
These fees are effective from 1 January 2018.
visa_types.processing_time:
Normal case processing times
Applications for au pair permits will be processed within eight weeks of the application being submitted to the police or a foreign service mission.
If it will take longer, we will notify you by email or SMS.
Which cases take longer to process?
In some cases, we need more information or documentation, or there may be things that we want to look into more closely. We would not do so if we did not believe that it is absolutely necessary in order to make a correct decision in the case.
What can you do to help your case being processed as quickly as possible?
Make sure that your application is completed correctly and in full.
Make sure that you enclose all the necessary documents, see our checklists.
Make sure that your contact information, as well as your host family’s, is up-to-date.
It is also a good idea to make sure that your host family in Norway has power of attorney from you, so that we can give important information to the
visa_types.forms:
The application form for Norway’s visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/)
Proof of consent by one parent if you are travelling with the other
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to come to Norway as an Au Pair, you need a residence permit. As an au pair, you can improve your language skills and learn about Norwegian society by living with a Norwegian family. In return, you will provide services such as light housework and/or child care for the host family.
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information or visit the websites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and The Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/).
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi, which is responsible for the further examination.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to come to Norway as an Au Pair, you need a residence permit. As an au pair, you can improve your language skills and learn about Norwegian society by living with a Norwegian family. In return, you will provide services such as light housework and/or child care for the host family.
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information or visit the websites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and The Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/).
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi, which is responsible for the further examination.

## Visa Fees
When you apply for a visa or residence permit to Norway, you must pay the fee online when registering your application at the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/). The below fee information is therefore provided for information purposes only.
Please note that applicants will also be charged a service fee of EUR 20 or QAR 84 per application which must be paid in cash when you attend the visa application centre. The service fee is liable to change in line with the European Commission exchange rate.

[nor > qat > en > study]
Visa fees can only be paid with a credit card through the Application Portal Norway.
These fees are effective from 1 January 2018.

## Documents Required

For information about the documents required, please visit the website of the Norway Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explains-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application) and go to the website of The Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/).
Please be advised that it is a requirement that all documents are submitted in original in the A4 format. Any government issued documents that you provide originals for (Nikah nama, birth certificates etc) must be accompanied by a copy. Any documents not in A4 size need to be copied into A4. The copies will have to be of very good quality with no staples, paper clips or glue as they will be scanned by the Embassy.
We stress the importance of submitting all the relevant documents. Missing documents may lead to a rejection or longer processing time. If you for any reason are not able to submit any of the documents, please write a short statement explaining this.
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in Abu Dhabi, which is responsible for the further examination.
Applying with children?
Please note that the Embassy of Norway will not be able to process the visa application if the child's photo on the passport is outdated or does not correspond with the recent photo enclosed.

## Photo Specifications
The photo must:
- be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- be printed on high quality photo paper with high solution
- be in colour
- be 35–40 mm in width
- be maximum 6 months old and resemble the applicant
- be taken with the applicant facing the camera directly, against a light background
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face must occupy 70–80 % of the picture.
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair must not cover the eyes.
Regarding glasses:
Glasses are allowed as long as the eyes are clearly visible.
- the glasses must not be coloured
- the rim must not cover any part of the eyes
- there must be no reflection in the glasses
Regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed, provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- shadows on the face due to the head-dress are not allowed
- Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. A photo booth for meeting these requirements is available at the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
Normal case processing times
Applications for au pair permits will be processed within eight weeks of the application being submitted to the police or a foreign service mission.
If it will take longer, we will notify you by email or SMS.
Which cases take longer to process?
In some cases, we need more information or documentation, or there may be things that we want to look into more closely. We would not do so if we did not believe that it is absolutely necessary in order to make a correct decision in the case.
What can you do to help your case being processed as quickly as possible?
Make sure that your application is completed correctly and in full.
Make sure that you enclose all the necessary documents, see our checklists.
Make sure that your contact information, as well as your host family’s, is up-to-date.
It is also a good idea to make sure that your host family in Norway has power of attorney from you, so that we can give important information to the

## Download Forms
The application form for Norway’s visa needs to be filled online. To launch an online application form, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://www.udi.no/en/)
Proof of consent by one parent if you are travelling with the other

Overview
This visa is granted to those who are visiting a family member who is an EU/EEA national. You must be able to provide proof that you fall under this category.
visa_types.full_text:
